steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes the three perspectives in Kim's novel — one from the mother, one from the father, one from the daughter. The word must precisely capture that each perspective belongs to a different, clearly separate person.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — distinct: "Distinct" means recognizably different or separate, which fits exactly — each perspective comes from a different family member. ✓
  • Choice B — unknown: The passage presents all three perspectives clearly and names each narrator; none are unknown. This contradicts the text. ✗
  • Choice C — unintended: The novel's structure — three deliberate sections, each named for a different character — is clearly intentional. "Unintended" contradicts this. ✗
  • Choice D — required: "Required" says something external demands these perspectives; the passage gives no indication of that. It doesn't address obligation or necessity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
